close_reason: Fixed in commit 8a284ca (add 30s HTTP timeout to doppler and gcp-sm clients)
description:
src/lib.rs:97 — `reqwest::Client::new()` uses default timeouts. reqwest's defaults are: no overall request timeout, no connect timeout. A hung Doppler endpoint, a half-open TCP connection that never completes a TLS handshake, or a load balancer that stops sending bytes mid-response will leave the get/put future pending forever.

Trigger: any network condition that produces a half-dead socket (firewall blackholing, packet-loss-induced TCP stalls, a Doppler frontend hung mid-handshake). Tokio runtime workers will accumulate hung tasks; if the consumer wraps this in a daemon, capacity drains.

Bad behavior: the SecretError::Unavailable mapping at src/lib.rs:148 only triggers on a reqwest::Error returning from .send().await. If .send() never returns, no error is produced and no retry layer above can intervene.

Fix: build the client once with reqwest::Client::builder().timeout(Duration::from_secs(30)).connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)).build() (numbers are illustrative; pick what's right for this workspace). Sibling backends with similar issues should also be checked — flag here only because the bug is present in this crate.

Note: the existing 'Note from Hyrum's Law' is that this changes observable behavior (timeouts now fire), but currently nobody can be relying on the unbounded behavior because nothing successfully completes a hang.
title: reqwest::Client::new() has no timeout — get/put can hang indefinitely on stalled connections
